operation: First, a closed 2D profile is sketched on the base workplane: draw a straight line from the origin to (20, 0), add a three-point arc passing through (25, 5) that ends at (18.11, 9.615), add a second three-point arc passing through (5, 6.266) that ends at (0, 6), then close the profile back to the origin. This profile is revolved 360 degrees to create a solid of revolution. Next, the face at the lowest Y position is selected, a new workplane is created on this face, an 8-sided construction polygon with a 55 unit circumscribed diameter is drawn, and 14 unit diameter through holes are cut at each of the 8 vertices of the construction polygon.
geometry: This is an axisymmetric solid with a smooth, curved, rounded outer profile similar to a thick contoured disc. Eight identical cylindrical through holes are arranged in a regular, evenly spaced circular pattern concentric with the solid's central rotational axis, with all holes oriented parallel to this central axis.
function: This is a mechanical adjustment knob or mounting coupling component, featuring a contoured grip-friendly outer surface and mounting holes for secure fastening to a shaft or mating part, intended for manual adjustment or mechanical connection use cases.
